Carracci is... dead?
A car bomb detonated right as he was walking by.
Carracci and three bodyguards near him died instantly.
Based on the wreckage at the scene, there was no point even taking him to the hospital.
I'm sorry.
The people who attacked us were without a doubt the same people who murdered Ministro Carracci. What could their objective be...?
Why are you here? There are so many court cars here as well—
You should've told me sooner that Bellone invited her here.
Cellinia Texas, as the first witness to the scene of Ministro Carracci's murder, you are also a suspect.
Now, I'm here to detain you.
Any resistance will be treated as flouting the laws of Siracusa, and will be punished accordingly.
Texas does not resist. She understands what she sees in the judge's eyes.
Moreover...
To all officials and associates of the famiglie, Ministro Carracci's importance to Volsinii is self-evident.
The attacks carried out on each of you will be investigated as part of the same investigation.
I, Lavinia Falcone, swear upon my honor to find the identity of the murderer. This shall not go unpunished!
You're not serious, are you?
Of course I am.
...Alright.
The string of events that just occurred makes Leontuzzo feel a bit fatigued.
And right as this thought crosses his mind, his entire world starts to spin.
Leon!
The rain continues to fall, with no signs of relenting.
Where am I...
Oh, you're finally awake.
This is... home?
Demetri, why are you here?
Lavinia brought you back.
According to the doctor, you passed out due to a combination of your injuries and fatigue.
Tch.
Want an apple?
Sure.
I knew you'd be hungry, so I already peeled it for you.
Where's Lavinia?
She left after delivering you to the guards.
You should know by now that she's always been reluctant to set foot inside this mansion.
Her coming here for your sake is already a miracle in itself.
Carracci's death is actually a bit of an issue.
And news of your injury is sure to get out.
In the eyes of the other famiglie, the Bellones have, in a heartbeat, gone from the clear frontrunner to a total mess, both inside and out.
Someone could show up at any moment to try to take a bite out of us.
Did we keep any of the assailants alive long enough to get them to talk?
Unfortunately, no.
And we couldn't find any leads on their bodies either.
I expected as much.
Any idea who could be behind this?
The Saluzzos always kept their cards close to their chests, biding their time. For them, this is a prime opportunity.
As for the Rossatis... They're probably victims too, since Wallach was with me when they attacked.
Still, Columbia's famiglie have never been a monolith.
True, there's always the possibility of infighting among them.
Besides, there are even more possibilities.
Tch, they sure found themselves a nice opening.
Mind pouring me some water?
You bastard, barking out even more orders than usual when you're lying in bed.
Did... you really just pour me a cup of boiling water?
Is it my fault your order wasn't specific enough?
Alright fine, I know this is how you express your dissatisfaction with me. What is it this time?
It's about the arrangement with Texas. I can't agree to it.
Lavinia is a righteous person who would never frame an innocent. She has her own ideas.
That's not what I meant. She was supposed to be your sword.
When the boss handed her over to you, that came with the assurance that she would not endanger the famiglia.
You should've forged her into a razor-sharp blade.
But now, she's behind bars under Lavinia's watch.
You seriously can't tell that she's looking out for our famiglia's interests?
I'll admit, holding onto Texas after today would only make things more precarious for you.
That her surname has appeared once more in Siracusa... draws far too much attention.
In that sense, you have a point. From Lavinia's perspective, it's safer to have Texas in hand.
But how is the rest of our famiglia supposed to see this?
They'll start to think that Leontuzzo, the next don of Famiglia Bellone, has gotten addicted to playing house on top of the tablecloth. That you've gone soft.
That not only do you not know how to swing a knife, you got it taken away from you.
The Bellones already lost the advantage. No matter who comes looking for trouble, we need to fight back tooth and nail.
Leon, the development of Volsinii's new district is more than the humdrum business of il Ministero dei Lavori Pubblici. It should be under Bellone control.
So that's what you really wanted to say to me.
I also have my own plans, Demetri.
Let's hear them, then.
Alright, fratello. You are my trusted advisor.
When I got attacked, I already had the feeling that things weren't adding up. And after hearing about Carracci's death, that became even more clear.
You just asked me who I suspected could be behind this.
I don't actually think it's a Saluzzo or a Rossati.
Why's that?
The hitmen knew me all too well.
They knew exactly how to hit our Famiglia with speed and precision, while targeting me at the same time.
So, what you're saying is—
I'm going to lock myself indoors for the next few days.
I need to recover from my wounds.
If people think we're fumbling in disarray, then let them think that.
Until the true enemy shows their tail.
So you let Lavinia take Texas on purpose.
That's right.
As for how she wants to play her hand, that's for her to decide.
In the meantime, there will no doubt be a slew of attacks on our famiglia.
Pay close attention to whoever's scheming behind the scenes.
...Understood.
So, was that a good enough answer for you?
Of course.
Since you have a clear plan in mind, naturally I'll help you put it into action.
You are, after all, the boss here.
That's what I want to hear.
Don't let me down, Demetri.
Hello, Wallach? It's me.
Leon, I heard you were injured. How are you doing?
I'm fine.
We need to talk about what happened earlier today.
I'd imagine... you probably have something to ask me too. About that name.
...'Texas.'
Volsinii Prison
So, you were held up at the time.
Who was holding you up?
An old acquaintance.
Awfully convenient for them to show up just then, don't you think?
You're pretty used to being in this kind of situation, aren't you?
Back in Lungmen, I'd sometimes let myself get interrogated like this.
Lungmen, huh...
Anyway, I'd have no reason to do anything like that.
That's what they all say.
To be honest, though, I don't think you could've been the perpetrator, Cellinia. I can't imagine it would've done you any good.
Also, Leon contacted me beforehand and gave me a few reasons why I should trust you.
So that's why you showed up at a venue where so many important figures were present. It was originally prepared as a stage for you by Famiglia Bellone.
However, given the current circumstances, our best option is for me to keep you here.
You're protecting Leontuzzo.
I'm protecting you as well, Texas.
Also... this may divert the attention of the other famiglie. They will be speculating what Bellone's next move will be after getting hit like this.
And those suspicions... might loosen some of the fetters around my ankles.
Are you really a judge?
Why would you ask that?
You seem to know a lot about how the famiglie operate.
I am, in fact, a judge.
But to be good at this job in Siracusa, you need to know how the mafiosi keep themselves alive, even better than they do.
Even if I'd rather not know.
You misunderstood me. It's not at all surprising that a law enforcement official would know a thing or two about the people you're enforcing it on.
But what I meant was that judges like you didn't exist back in the old Siracusa.
Don't get the wrong idea, Cellinia Texas.
I didn't bring you here because of the so-called checks and balances among the famiglie. Outsiders may interpret my stance as such, but I couldn't care less.
You just arrived in this city, and you still don't understand how important Ministro Carracci really was.
He was an outstanding person. He could've made the new city under construction something far better.
It wasn't just another project to expand his business, either. He wanted to make it possible for the citizens to enjoy a new way of life.
But he's dead now.
And I will find his murderer and bring them to justice.
They think they can dispose of the people who refuse to bend the knee one after another, as if they were puppets.
But I will make them realize that they are not tossing away a broken doll. They are murdering living, flesh-and-blood people.
So, Carracci's death had nothing to do with you.
Right.
And you didn't find out who the murderer was.
Pretty much.
And the task I gave you, to investigate exactly why Bellone decided to bring back Texas? No leads there either?
You never miss a beat, my dear father.
And despite having accomplished nothing at all, you didn't even bother showing up when I called to convene a meeting.
The traffic is pretty bad this time of year.
Lappland, when I kicked you out of the house seven years ago, you lost your right to call yourself a Saluzzo. Do you understand what that means?
Of course I do. It means you won't show any mercy when you decide to dispose of a disobedient tool like me.
You've always been so self-aware, yet never have the slightest inclination to act accordingly.
Do you really need me to personally deliver a little warning to the poor little Judgie?
Why would I feel a need to warn her?
Now that the princeling pup is hiding behind closed doors, everybody's gonna think the sharp-tongued little Judgie is a Bellone puppet.
And with your personality, you aren't inclined to believe that the Bellones are as weak as they present.
So wouldn't it be common sense to use the judge's well-being to test the waters?
Seeing how you understand what to do, I want to see results.
Why naturally, I'd never disobey your orders. Assuming our interests align, that is.
My dearest father.
Salvadore! What are we to do now?!
Am I to thank you for freeing me from the clutches of my own blood relative? Am I to thank you for spilling blood so that the Infected workers may have full stomachs?
The man you killed was my father!
No, I do not need a list of his crimes. I should think I know them better than you. The scars on our bodies are proof of his cruelty. The deaths of those laborers were proof of his exploitation.
But still, despite it all!
Salvadore, I truly thank you from the bottom of my heart, for saving me and so many others.
But the stench of blood lingering about you grows stronger and stronger...
Let us be away from this place, Salvadore, just the two of us... Take me somewhere we will at last be able to find peace, alright?
It might already be too late.
Salvadore, it might already have been too late the day you first set foot in Columbia, from when you first sharpened your blade...
You say this was all destined? That it couldn't have been avoided?
I can't help but think... If I were in Vivienne's place, would I be able to forgive Salvadore?
Even if what he did could be forgiven, the grudge would follow the both of them for the rest of their lives.
Exactly right, signorina.
Huh?
That the two of them were able to get together in the end is, of course, artistic license.
In fact, the real Salvadore did not elope with Vivienne.

Also, despite the fact that Vivienne's father was indeed as callous as he was wealthy, he took good care of his family - particularly Vivienne, whom he doted on like a diamond in the palm of his hand.
Oh...
Very rarely is someone pure evil, don't you think?
Therefore, even if the perpetrator was someone you loved dearly, even if you were aware of your father's despicable acts, something like what he did is not so easily forgiven.
Signorina Vivienne ultimately chose to leave him and move to a new city, where she met someone who truly loved her and thus began a new life.
But, until her last breath, she never forgot the man named Salvadore.
So in a sense, this story can be seen as vicariously helping her dream come true.
Did you... know Ms Vivienne well?
Hmm... I suppose you could say that.
My name is Caterina. You're quite the lovely young actress.
Are you new here? I swing by frequently, but I've never seen your face before.
I am. My name is Sora, and I came from Lungmen.
Lungmen? How fascinating.
I've heard of many actors who went to Lungmen to develop their careers, but rarely the other way around.
Sometimes, there's value in bucking the trend.
Too true.
So Miss Caterina, do you know what happened to the actual Ms Vivienne?
Vivienne, huh... She never forgot Salvadore.
Maybe that's why her son chose a similar path— he became a wise guy, and gradually rose to become the don of a certain famiglia.
A famiglia that would eventually go on to clash against Salvadore himself.
Fortunately, Vivienne came forward and prevented the two rivals from becoming mortal enemies, and eventually they even became allies.
Wait, are you talking about the Rossati Family from the script?
That's right. Have you already read through the entire thing?
Yes... I also wanted to learn as much as I could about the Texas Family's history, for some personal reasons.
So what did you think of the story?
I... There are a few bits I can't make sense of.
Oh? For example?
For example, Salvadore's granddaughter...
Sora, the stylist wants you to take a look at the design of your costume!
Okay, I'll be right there!
Sorry, but I have to go.
I don't mind. We'll have another chance to talk.
Sora—
Take your time. We can always chat at our leisure some other day.
Okay, that's great!
Salvadore's granddaughter.
Heh.
You're wasting time at the theater again, boss.
How many times do I have to tell you, Wallach? It's not a waste of time.
You pushed most of your work onto me just so you could go write a shitty play? Is that what the boss of a famiglia should be doing? And you even gave yourself a pseudonym?
Do you have a problem with that?
Of course not. You are none other than Giovanna Rossati, our beloved Donna.
I know you're dissatisfied with me.
But—
Is there really anything in this city worth worrying over?
Before, no. But now, yes.
Carracci is dead.
Oh?
But Carracci was originally just a ordinary man, elevated by the famiglie to keep each other in check.
All his death proves is that Bellone was not capable enough, nothing more.
And someone greatly resembling Cellinia appeared at Leontuzzo's side.
Say that again?
Texas. Cellinia Texas.
Wallach, I don't recall you being the sort of man who liked to crack jokes.
And you should also know that I hate people who joke about Texas more than anything else.
So you can tell I'm not joking.
I have no idea how Bellone managed to find her, but she had those unmistakable eyes and hair.
Exactly like the one in your photo.
So she didn't die...
And she's with Leontuzzo now.
She would never have survived the liquidation if not for a certain famiglia protecting her.
...The logical conclusion is that Bellone saved her back then.
Looks like we're going to need to have a little chat.
Giovanna, this clearly is a trap—
Wallach.
...My apologies, boss.
It's not so much a 'trap' as an entire conspiracy.
Think bigger.
If they really were the ones who saved her back then, what good does it do me to drop by and express my gratitude?
Make preparations. 'Business discussions' are over. I'm paying Volsinii a visit.
Understood. I'll make the arrangements.
Alas, it seems that the new script will have to go on the backburner for a while.
Boss, I'm sure you're aware that Carracci's death presents an excellent opportunity for us.
But you still seem hung up on the Texas name.
If this keeps up, the Rossatis won't have a future.
Woooah, this dress looks absolutely stunning!
You'n say that again. Sora's gonna look mighty fine in it.
If someone told me about this earlier, I would've signed up myself!
No way, no how. Ya don't know the first thing about actin'.
I can just act like I always do.
And there ain't no roles for a Sankta in Siracusa neither!
Says who? I heard that there was a Sankta with Signora Sicilia—
Huh?
Signorina Sally, our boss is inviting you over for dinner.
Um... But...
She's on her way to rehearsal right now. Vaffanculo.
And our boss wants to have a word with Signorina Sally here. Being a little late won't make a difference, will it?
I, I'll go get the director...
The director? I might've carried water for him in the past, but these days...
Um, please don't...
The hell are you playin' nice with them for?
You should feel honored that our boss wants to see you. If you don't wanna get messed up, behave yourself and come with us.
Wait, the boss didn't say we could—
You still don't understand what the boss was gettin' at? We were all scared of the Bellones before.
But now that they are out of the picture, and the bigwigs are about to shuffle the deck again, we'll never get a better chance!
Huh, you're right. Let's go, brothers, time to clean the streets of these eyesores!
Tch... Protect Signorina Sally!
The heck's happenin', Exu— Huh? Where'd she go?
She already rushed out. Let's back her up!
Urgh...
Heh, and you call yourself a bodyguard?
Ahh... *sobs*...
Right this way, signorina.
I, I know...
Huh? You people are—
Who the hell are you?!
Catch me if you can.
Tch, get her!
Don't mess with us!
Let's split!
Your name's Sally, right? You okay?
You...
Do you know what you've done?!
You didn't save me at all, you're just making things worse! They're not going to let this go!
So how'd you plan to get out of that mess?
...By gritting my teeth.
Huh? But they were gonna hurt you.
I... can deal with injuries.
Did you really think they were going to kidnap me? They're just trying to cause trouble.
Shouldn't you at least call for help when stuff like this happens?
What's the point? Nobody's going to intervene in a struggle between famiglie.
What about that Judge Lavinia lady? She seemed like a pretty decent person to me.
She's... indeed a very good judge, but there's nothing she can do in these situations.
Besides, even if I never made it out of this park, how many people would know that I disappeared because of the conflict between those mafiosi?
So... you're just going to put up with it?
How else are we supposed to live our lives? This is how it's always been.
Find a place to hide, wait until the dust settles, and then follow the victorious side out. That's all I can do.
But... why does it have to be like that?
Ugh, I'm sorry. I'm not trying to blame you, but to be honest, this is the first time in my life I've ever known what it feels like to be protected.
That famiglia isn't even particularly influential. The director should be able to help me smooth things over.
But just as a friendly word of advice, if you plan to stay alive in Siracusa, please try to avoid doing that kind of stuff in the future.
Especially considering what's about to happen.
What's about to happen?
Have you not seen the news at all? Carracci, Ministro dei Lavori Pubblici, is dead.
Yeah, saw that. Assassinated, looks like?
I feel so bad for him.
Since you all seem nice enough, I'll tell you a little more about him.
I have to deal with mafiosi pretty regularly, so I've heard a thing or two from them.
The person who murdered him hasn't been found yet, and the young master of Famiglia Bellone was also attacked at the same time.
Huh? Bellone?!
That's right. The Bellones were poised to stand head and shoulders above the other famiglie, but it seems like someone's been plotting against them for a while now.
Many famiglie that had previously given up are now starting to move again, little by little.
Did you hear any of them mention the name Texas?
Texas? There's not a single person in Siracusa who doesn't know that name.
But usually in the context of 'the last Texas' or something along those lines...
Really?!
You're a strange one, you know that? It's obviously all made-up. How could there possibly be a Texas still alive out there?
But... Alright, you have a point.
In any case, you should already know that our troupe has some connections to Famiglia Bellone, through the director.
Just as those people were saying, in the past everyone wanted to get on his good side. But now...
You all saw what happened.
Do all of Siracusa's famiglie go around doing whatever the hell they want like that?
...Remember what I'm about to tell you.
If you want to survive in Siracusa, you either need to join a famiglia, or stay as far away from them as possible.
Then... you need to delude yourself into believing that as long as you stay away from them, you won't get sucked into any trouble.
Alright, I should head back to the theater now.
Right.
Aaaaaaaahhhh—!
What now?!
S-Someone's been tied up over here!
Assuming nothin' outta the ordinary happened, someone ought to have found the poor fella by now.
I don't get it, man. Why go through all that trouble keeping him alive, just to let him go? What's so special about that guy anyway?
What it proves is, that shady bartender wanted him to be found.
As for why... it's usually a trap.
Tch, what a sinister fella.
You say that now, but if he ends up winning, you're gonna be calling him a genius.
Dipshit.
Hah.
What's so funny?
I just remembered how we used to argue like this back when we were kids.
Never would've imagined that after all these years, after all of life's twists and turns, the two of us'd still be out here, fightin' for our lives.
That's fate for you. Never know where it's gonna take you.
Heh. Ain't that the truth.
Who could've known something like that would happen in Lungmen?
And that after all that, we'd still come back to Siracusa.
Gambino... If, and I mean if, Lappland has really decided to leave us alone, and let us fend for ourselves...
Would you still stay in Siracusa?
What, do you miss your Lungmen that bad?
Hmph. Forget it, not like you'd ever understand anyway.
Let's talk about something more practical. You really plan on switching employers?
Looking at the news, the guy we tried to off today was Don Bellone's son. Seems like someone real important died at the same time.
Which sounds to me an awful lot like some unknown famiglia has been using us as their weapons.
Might as well go make that relationship formal, then.
Better than taking on odd jobs and begging for scraps.
That's right. As soon as you're back in Siracusa, you participate in an attempted assassination of one of the Dodici dons. You two sure have grown.
Guess the good ol' days are over...
Lappland...
Oh? Today's breakfast is looking pretty good.
Pay for your own.
Aw, is that any way to treat your boss?
So, what've you been up to all this time?
I went to say hi to the folks, and got to enjoy some sorely-missed family time. Simple as that.
Haah, what a relief that despicable old mug back home is just as punchable as it's always been.
You with a famiglia?
Oh, did I never mention it? My last name is Saluzzo.
Saluzzo?
You're a Saluzzo?
Capone, do you know what the thing I hate the third most is?
Capone feels a chill course through his body, but the sensation is so fleeting that he has to wonder if it was his imagination.
When people start wailing and moaning once they hear the name of my famiglia.
Ah, come to think of it, the way you do things really does remind me of that one famiglia.
What about the other two things? If I'm gonna die, might as well die knowing.
Haha. Seems like you've picked up on how to get along with me.
Actually, I just came up with that line on the spot.
Tch.
Alright, talk to me. So why'd the two of you decide to do a hit on Famiglia Bellone?
Come in.
Sorry for bothering you, Signor Segretario.
No, no, Your Honor, the pleasure is mine.
Your office is... quite impressive.
Haha, I frequently have distinguished guests here, and this is the sort of decor they prefer, so I figured I'd go along with it.
So, did you need me for something?
I wanted to talk to you about Carracci's death.
Hmm? I believe I already made a statement about that.
I know.
But I wanted to have a chat with you, for more personal reasons.
Oh right... I heard you swore you'd find Carracci's murderer and bring them to justice.
Good, good. Young folks ought to have that kind of drive.
So have you made any progress?
Unfortunately, not yet.
Sorry to hear that.
I heard you'd always been on good terms with him personally, so...
That's true. Carracci was greatly concerned with the citizenry's well being, things such as food, water, and sanitation.
But I don't think there's anything I can do to assist you.
I never actually mingled my way into his innermost circle.
So while I'd love to help, there's nothing I can do.
Anything you can give me is fine. A place you think is suspicious, or anyone in the government I should keep an eye on.
You're asking too much. You understand what I'm saying, don't you?
Your Honor... several other Ministri dei Lavori Pubblici died before Carracci. Do you remember them?
Seven? Or maybe eight?
The people in those positions were just puppets. Each famiglia was trying to push their own people up there.
But in the end, they were all taken out by other famiglie.

When it came to a position as important as il Ministro dei Lavori Pubblici, though, I'll be blunt here, it was clear as day why the famiglie filled the position with a man as spotless as Carracci instead of installing one of their cronies.
For one simple reason: if the killing continued, the construction of the city would grind to a halt.
'If our people can't get in, then we'll at least make sure yours can't either.' That is why Carracci occupied that seat.
Make no mistake, he had close ties with the Bellones. The Don's son even sat beside him as his secretary. But, in one way or another, he was just as close with every other famiglia.

There's an ancient Yan saying to the effect of 'he who walks oft along the river, inevitably finds his shoes wet,' yes? I have always trusted in Carracci's integrity, but so what? Others did not see it that way.
The way Siracusans think, you either choose to be one famiglia's pet, or you choose to be another's.
In other words, Carracci's death was inevitable. He wasn't part of any of famiglia's vision for the future. Do you think he was oblivious to that fact?
In the end, it doesn't matter whether he died today, a month ago, or a month from now.
You know how much hard work he put in.
And what did he get for it in the end?
Actually, let's assume he was as selfless a person as you say. What did the people he worked so hard for, those whom he so eloquently claimed as 'his dear citizens,' give him in the end?
Your Honor, I've also heard a great many rumors about you.
You undeniably have a fair number of accomplishments under your belt.
But among them, how many were due solely to your own hard work, and how many were influenced by the people behind your back? You already know the answer, don't you?
You—
If I may be frank, Your Honor, are you really not here to test me on behalf of Bellone?
That is not my intention, I swear.
...I'd prefer it were.
Then please heed this word of caution, Your Honor.
Let those who've died stay dead.
We already have too much to worry about without overly concerning ourselves with the deceased.
And... this is the attitude of someone who was on good terms with him?
If you want to have a slightly longer lease on life in Volsinii's government, I'd suggest you learn this lesson well.
Oh, hello sweetheart. Ah, I'm still at work right now. Are you ready to go?
Mm, alright. I'll get ready to head over then.
Okay, okay.
That was my daughter. Now, a newly-opened restaurant has invited me to inspect their ingredients tonight.
We'll have to set aside our discussion for later.
Oh right, please let Master Leontuzzo know that he is welcome to talk to me if he's interested in the gourmet experience.
...I'll remember to pass it on.
Now then, please see yourself out.
...*sigh*.
What did you say? You found someone unconscious and tied up? The leader of the bunch that attacked the restaurant?
Understood. I'll be there right away.
I'm telling you, the two of us just happened to be involved in that hit. We don't know nothing.
As for the guy who handed out the orders, we don't know which famiglia he belongs to, but he sure was generous with the cash.
They were clearly looking for hitmen with murky backgrounds like you two, so when you die, there'd be no trail back to who hired you.
You might think you ran into them by chance, but it wouldn't surprise me if they had their eyes on you a long time ago.
I'm not saying they didn't.
So, want to take a crack at who your employer is?

Probably... the Rossatis or the Saluzzos. Of the Dodici who've been fighting over this city all this time, and with the Bellones out of the picture, they're the only two famiglie with enough power left.
Right, I'm sure most people would think the same.
But now you know the Saluzzos were never planning anything like that.
That just leaves... the Rossatis?
One more.
What do you mean?
Remember those poor suckers you took out early on? They were originally targeting people from il Ministero dei Lavori Pubblici.
Don Bellone's son's been working there, so another way of putting this is that the folks you dealt with were trying to get in Bellone's way.
So... what you're saying is, we helped the Bellones put down a threat, but then turned right around and hit 'em even harder.
...So what? We see famiglie doing that kinda power jockeying stuff every day.
That... He's got a point. The first hit we made might've just been on some unlucky, short-sighted bastards.
But don't forget, you were specifically sent to 'shut them up.'
Wait, are you serious?!
Are you suggesting... there's a double agent inside the Bellones?
Just another possibility.
Hahaha, but hey, that's what makes things interesting.
Let's assume that's true and do a little thought experiment here.
Carracci, il Ministro dei Lavori Pubblici, is dead. And before that, several more people in the ministero were killed.
Naturally, most people are going to see those things as connected, especially that poor judge.
But. The perpetrator who carried out the hit has already been silenced, and she can't find their leader either. She's at a complete loss, so what does she do next?
If only some kind of clue would appear.
And what do you know, isn't that assassin you dumped out there the best possible lead?
So that's why they waited until now to throw that guy out...
It does make sense, when you put it that way.
Then, they set up a little ambush, and make sure the judge has an 'accident'...
Kaboom.
Lappland stretches out her hands and falls back onto the sofa, as if knocked back by the blast.
Everyone's gonna get pulled in.
Ahh, what a great idea.
It's so great that even if this is all bullshit, I'd want to make it reality.
So... lemme get this straight, you two are sayin' that the Bellones are puttin' on a whole goddamn opera?
It's definitely possible.
After all, nobody understands the Bellones better than themselves.
Hmph. I don't buy it.
These are the famiglia's plans for the future we're talking about. There ain't a single wise guy in all of Siracusa who'd attack his own famiglia at a time like this.
Gambino, you should've realized by now that kinda blind confidence is what brought down the Sicilians.
You—
But I gotta say, I think I agree with you this time.
Aha! Then maybe they're putting on this show to feign weakness, so everyone thinks they're vulnerable and—
But they were already on the cusp of victory.
Do you seriously think that the Bellones just decided to give up on a city that was right at their fingertips, just to turn this whole place upside-down?
Are they crazy or something?
Who knows? There are more lunatics walking this land than you might think.
Haha, but I guess our merry mealtime needs to wrap up.
I hope you have a jolly ol' time out there.
You stopped by just to say hello?
What, are you that eager to be my henchmen?
I wouldn't say it's high on my priorities.
But at least tell us which side you're on.
Last thing I want is to end up fighting against you without realizing, and dying without knowing what hit me.
Me? Naturally, I'll always be Texas's most loyal supporter.
Is everyone in position for the ambush?
...Yes.
You seem like you want to say something.
I was just thinking... I feel bad for Signorina Lavinia.
Whatever the case may be, she's a friend to the famiglia.
If only the one she befriended wasn't Leon, the next don of Famiglia Bellone—
Look, I don't want to be the villain either.
So she wants to play her little game of justice. Sure, that we can allow, to an extent.
But she thinks she has the backing of one of the bosses. What she's done over the years has had a larger and larger impact on Leon... No, on our entire famiglia.
The Bellones don't need softness like that.
She must be made crystal-clear of this fact, and so must Leon.
Otherwise...
We'll never be able to seize ultimate victory from the chaos that's about to ensue.
But, the young master, he—
I know many of you are still conflicted about this. I will assume full responsibility for whatever happens. When Leon eventually comes asking who to blame, I won't get any of you involved.
...*sigh*.

lines.st: Cellinia, I need to send you to stay in Siracusa for a while.
You have to go.

The Columbian famiglie changed into opulent attire, took up residence in luxurious mansions, and stepped into the so-called upper class, thinking they had clearly delineated a boundary between them and Siracusa.
'Siracusa is barbaric, while we are not.' They spoke those words, thinking themselves superior to our homeland.
Your father's been captured by this line of thinking too.
As for you, I need you to see for yourself.
See the place we came from. See if we're any different from them.

lines.x: ...Who's there?
I've brought you some chocolate. Want some?
I see prison security in Siracusa is as shoddy as ever.
You know, they call this one impregnable, at least compared to other cities.
Texas lets out a tiny sigh, and takes a bite out of the chocolate in Lappland's hand.
It's her least favorite flavor.
Hah. And here I thought you'd be chasing me out instead.
That takes a lot more effort than just ignoring you.
So long as you don't get in my way, do whatever you want.
Aw, I'm almost moved to tears here, Texas. Even you're gradually figuring out how to get along with me.
I never get in other peoples' way. I'd much rather clear the fog for them, so they can better see where they're going.
How much do the Saluzzos know?
No more than you do.
The Bellones were pretty much a shoo-in to win, but to keep the Rossatis down, they even brought you back from Lungmen, as a precautionary measure of sorts.
But right at the critical moment, yesterday's assassination took place.
It's so blatant that people can't help but think it had to be intentional. Is the perpetrator even trying to stay hidden?
So what about you? Planning to sit in this cell reminiscing about every little thing that happened in Lungmen until that judge lets you go?
Weren't you in a hurry to get back there?
If you consider the wait as just another leg of the journey home, then I can be pretty patient.
But what if that judge is in danger?
Where is he? Has he woken up yet?
Good to see you here, Your Honor! We've already taken full control of the scene.
The suspect is still unconscious, but he was tied up and stuffed into a trash can over by the corner. We've already notified the hospital.
Who found him?
A group of actors from Compagnia dell'alba. They already left because they had a rehearsal to get to.
Compagnia dell'alba... Bernardo's people?
He really is cursed with bad luck.
Your Honor, the people murdered in the witness elimination case you reported last time were this person's accomplices, right?
Yes, but we couldn't find anything on them to identify them with.
Why would their leader show up here in such a sorry state then...?
Want me to try waking him up?
Please do.
*cough* *cough*... *cough* *cough*!
Stop struggling. We already have you restrained.
...Lavinia.
Do you understand the situation you're in? Who tied you up and dumped you here?
Do you really need to ask, judge?
Answer my question, murderer.
Heh. Maybe I can invoke Article 14 of the penal code to remain silent under informal investigations.
...You're pretty familiar with the law.
What, are you gonna use Article 132, the right of special dispensation, on me? Judge?
Just who are you?
Your average everyday murderer.
I, I remember now... I've seen you before.
Last night at the restaurant, no? You rescued that hapless clerk from il Ministero dei Lavori Pubblici.
It was three years ago, at a legal forum. Judge Bortolotti from the city of Sorrento gave a particularly outstanding speech.
Signor, I was deeply impressed by your report.
Well, I just kill people for a living now. It's an easy life. What you're talking about, I can barely even remember anymore.
This isn't who you are.
Stop acting so self-righteous, Lavinia. You really think you understand me? Just from a fantasy-filled report at a window-dressing legal forum?
You can't hide true passion. That's what you said back then.
Right, along with all the other naive, idealistic nonsense I rattled off. All young people are like that, thinking they can change the world all on their own.
I just happened to wake up sooner than you, Lavinia.
You said, 'we should all strive to find the common line that can protect anyone.'
Yes, here's an example: join a famiglia, listen to the boss, and kill anyone who gets in your way.
Judge Bortolotti, I can help you.
Hah! Help me?!
Oh, that's right. You have the great and powerful Bellones backing you.
Your honor and dignity will stay untarnished. You won't get chained down or beaten up for a moment of naivete. You won't come home to the sight of your house going up in smoke along with your family.
You don't know a damn thing, Judge Lavinia.
You have no idea how weighty the code you hold in your hand is, and how easily it can destroy your life.
And luckily for you, you'll never have a chance to find out.
Watch out, Lavinia!
What...?
We need to get out of here, now.
Texas?
They've sent a ton of people. Come with me.
Why is she here...?
It's T-Texas!
We've already lost a lot of our men!
Tch, we shouldn't have let Lavinia talk to that piece of trash for so long.
Forget it. After seeing her colleague fall to utter depravity, that woman's spirit is done for.
As long as we can snuff out Leon's childishness, it doesn't matter how we do it.
Call our people back. We're leaving.
They... They turned around and left!
He's already dead. Took an arrow straight through the heart.
Thank you. If not for you all, I'd have been the one to die here.
Close your eyes, murderer.
What's this...?
A notebook?
A Siracusan judge license. He was keeping it in his breast pocket.
Its corner has been clipped, and it's written here that the holder was disbarred three years ago for malfeasance.
Malfeasance, huh. Malfeasance.
Could they really not come up with a better excuse?
Are you alright?
I... think I should be asking you how you got out of prison.
Trade secret.

In Siracusa, the courts stand for Grey Hall's will.
And the judges are executors of Signora Sicilia's will.
The judges inevitably find themselves controlled by the famiglie, sometimes even submitting to them. However, every judge knows there is one line that cannot be crossed—
They must prevent fights between the Dodici Famiglie.
Just as the Dodici all know their every move is always under the watchful eye of Signora Sicilia.
Thus, a delicate balance of power is established through the courts.
But now—
A declaration of contempt for Signora Sicilia.
A mockery of the very laws of Siracusa.
With an engine's roar squeezed between bouts of high-pitched laughter...
A truck rips through the wall.
And even after crashing its way inside, the engine does not stop.
As if proclaiming to others—
This is no mere accident, but rather by design.
Aaaaaaahhh—
Wh-What's going on?! What's a truck doing here?!
Who's the driver? Get out of there this instant!
Heh, not bad. Now that's the face I wanted to see.
Who the hell are you?!
Ah, yes. Buongiorno, signore e signori.
Allow me to introduce myself.
My name... is Lappland Saluzzo.
Saluzzo?!

I am also...
Carracci's true assassin.
Breaking news!
Just now, someone drove a truck into the courtroom.
The woman who carried out this jaw-dropping deed just identified herself as Lappland Saluzzo.
And, she claims to be the one who really assassinated Carracci!
At present, Judge Lavinia has suspended the trial and detained this individual.
We will continue to track the latest developments, so don't go anywhere—
When people no longer recognize the stench of blood as blood, it becomes a natural part of society.
Let this be a poignant wake-up call.
In a city where even the sewers are choked with conspiracy and spilled blood, such fascinating developments can still take place.
O wolf with a yearning for the wilderness, you never fail to surprise.
But will you break free of your shackles? That, I do wish to see.
Over a dozen mobsters lie on the ground, howling.
The only one still standing is a girl holding a longbow.
I never knew city wolves were this weak.
Agnese was right.
She pauses briefly, then suddenly turns to sniff the air.
It's coming from the direction of the warehouse.
Are there other wolves from the wilds in this city?
No... This is not the scent of the wilderness.
But, it's very close to it.
No one told me about this!
Me neither.
Did she act on her own?
Don't worry about it.
I chose to let her do it.
Don Alberto! But why?
A gold coin can mean very different things in different circumstances.
The Bellones will not have their way.
What's a little smirch on our reputation, if it means we cut another piece of flesh off Bernardo?
Understood.
What should we do now?
Prepare my car. I'm going to see my daughter.
Yes, Don Alberto.
By the way, where is Danbrown now?
Danbrown? I think he's working at a carwash.
Give me the address.
Are you calling him back?
I never permitted him to leave the famiglia.
Understood.
And prepare a banquet.
For...?
A feast for the heir to Famiglia Bellone.
Let me go!
We caught the assassins, thanks to Lappland's tip-off.
They've been identified as having taken part in Leontuzzo's assassination.
Do you two know her?
Hey.
Nothing good ever comes of dealing with you, Lappland.
Look on the bright side. It's not every day that you get to sit in a Siracusan cell.
Lock them up.
Yes, Your Honor.
You were the one who waylaid Cellinia, and ordered the bombing of Carracci...
Is that still your story?
You sure are persistent, aren't you?
Don't you and Bellone want to get this case closed as quickly as you can?
Does it matter if it was me or Texas who did it?
You really should hang me, Your Honor.
I'm just a pawn my famiglia cast aside. Surely, you can do it.
Imagine the positive impact that my death will have on justice in Siracusa.
Isn't it worth a try?
I don't know what you're planning.
But I'll do exactly that, if you really are the culprit, Lappland Saluzzo.
First, there's someone I have to see.
Welcome back, Don Bernardo.
Take a break. You've been working hard.
Yes, Don Bernardo.
Not happy?
How could I be?
Come with me.
There are some things that you have to know.
Why did you bring me here?
My lord.
Bernardo. Are you asking me to tame your unruly cub now?
He just needs to understand something.
What are you—
I am il Signore dei Lupi, cub.
Leontuzzo feels a daze at the words 'Signore dei Lupi'.
Fragments of knowledge and memory rush into his mind and take shape.
The Signori dei Lupi. Sovereign of beasts.
Beings that were never born. They have existed since time immemorial.
Zaaro is a sovereign of beasts.
He and his kind have borne witness to the path that the wolfpack tread on the wildness.
They are engaged in a killing game, guided by their own nature.
The Signori dei Lupi are immortal.
They gain no satisfaction from fighting amongst themselves, so they have set rules for their game.
Each Signore dei Lupi grooms a human to hunt the others.
These are the Fangs of the Signore dei Lupi.
The one with the last Fang standing becomes the alpha.
Signore dei Lupi, the hunt, Fangs...
Could it be...?
Your father has been an excellent Fang.
Rubio, Don Alberto is holding a banquet for Famiglia Bellone.
We'll need the best food.
Could it really be Saluzzo?
Rubio?
Oh, it's nothing.
Let me think... ordinary hospitality won't do when the Bellones are involved.
Please inform Don Alberto that it would be my honor to prepare the banquet, personally.
I eagerly await his response.
In Columbia, a show of kindness by a mafioso often results in the subject becoming a chip in a bargain.
The more one cares, the higher the cost.
In Siracusa, the subject may take their last breath on a dark, rainy street.
The more one cares, the greater the pain.
One must become cold and heartless.
Texas knows that well, and detests it.
I'm sorry.
No need to apologize to me.
You can leave after some paperwork, if you want.
Procedurally, you're no longer a suspect.
I'm not worried. It's quieter here in jail.
Haven't you given up yet?
Do you really think your old friend did it?
It's her style, but I don't see the motive.
Then it's not time to give up.
Signorina Lavinia, Famiglia Rossati sends word that Signor Wallach would like to speak with Signorina Cellinia.
Well... no rest for the wicked, then.
Lavinia realizes that Texas put something in her pocket as she passed by.
She reaches into it and finds a note.
There is a phone number and a short message scribbled in a corner:
'Penguin Logistics delivers, rain or shine.'
Signor, if you have to sleep, can you at least put your legs down?
...Hmm?
Are you talking to me?
Who else? How could you sleep in the theater?
And to put your feet up like that! How rude!
I'm not blocking you, am I?
No, but it's really off-putting to have someone so rude beside me!
Alright, I'll put my legs down.
Hey, why do you have food with you?
We don't sell food here.
Yeah, I bought it at the corner store, since you don't sell popcorn here.
Is it your first time here?
A friend suggested I come here to relax.
What do you think of the performance?
I don't really understand what's going on.
Why are they doing that?
Escort this gentleman out.
He's not welcome here.
Hey, I bought a ticket!
Give him a refund.
Right away.
Come with us, signor.
And get the hell out.
Say, I saw him when the show started.
I thought he was some rich boy out to experience society.
Turns out it was a poor cazzo who knows nothing about opera.
Did you like what they were doing on stage?
Of course. The actors looked nice.
And the songs were beautiful.
Is that what they call 'art'?
Stick to the bars, pleb.
Oh yeah, here's your ticket money.
Don't you dare say we took it.
What's that? You wanna do this the hard way?
Hey, this guy's strong.
I need some help here!
Something's not right with his eyes.
A-Aaaahh! My arm! It's gonna break!
H-Hold it right there!
Just let it go, don't waste your time with a cazzo like that.
C'mon, let's get you bandaged up.
Sorry, Signorina Lavinia...
Doesn't look like this is something for me.
Guess I should find a bar to get myself drunk, then spend the night at the porno theater.
Want a drink?
Danbrown looks in the direction the voice came from.
He sees a man leaning by the side of the alley, shaking a bottle in his hand.
I want an explanation, Lappland.
Isn't this what you wanted?
Help the Bellones when they're at their weakest, control them, and dominate them, rather than destroy them.
I thought I taught you.
I taught you what to do in times like this.
Oh, I forgot. It's been years since I left, you know.
I can have you ignoring the business I give you, or you going against me.
You're my daughter, after all.
I'm a forgiving man, but I can't let you go over my head to make decisions for the famiglia.
Even if you did actually guess what I wanted to do.
I am don of our famiglia.
Seven years ago, I kicked you out for this. Seven years later, you came back and did it again.
You knew it would infuriate me. You knew it was the only thing that could infuriate me.
Why did you do it, figlia mia?
Because nothing else brings me joy, padre mio.
I wanted to know if it's really your position that matters most.
I've always wanted to know.
Lappland raises herself from the ground with great effort.
Her face is full of joy, as though this was what she came back to Siracusa for.
Time's up, boss.
That'll be all for today.
That's all?
You defied me.
But you're still my daughter.
You know what I want, better than anyone.
All you're missing is discipline.
It's my fault for not educating you properly.
But there's still time.
So all the Signori dei Lupi live amongst us?
No, he's the only one who involves himself in human society.
The rest of them stay out in the wildness.
Why?
He believes that the authority over man is a form of power.
And he wants to use it to triumph over the other Signori dei Lupi.
So he wants you to overthrow Signora Sicilia, become the de facto ruler of Siracusa, and help him win his fight with his kind?
You, me, the entire famiglia, we're all tools to him.
Did you think I was going to accept that?
How could you accept being a puppet your entire life?!
Figlio mio, there's one thing you've misunderstood.
He taught me how to win power, just as I taught you.
Using it to defeat the other Fangs is just a part.
Every decision that I made, including what I'm doing now, is my own.

lines.st: How can you be so sure?

lines.x: (No, he's serious.)
(Look at him, the strength of his character, he's no puppet.)
We're each other's tools, instruments for our mutual victory. That's all.
Boss, the Saluzzos have sent their invitation.
Don Alberto would like Signor Leon to attend his banquet.
Sigh.
I've lost count of how many times you sighed today, Sora.
Can't be helped, considering what happened at the trial.
How about we raid the prison?
Ya lost yer darned mind?
At least we'd get to see her.
I wouldn't recommend it.
What are you doing here, Miss Caterina?
That's my line, Sora. Why are you out here? It's dangerous.
I live nearby.
You do? Did you get scammed?
It's fine, she's got me n' Exusiai.
Who's this, Sora?
Oh, I met Caterina a few days ago over at the theater.
Well... I guess it's fine, if you have friends you can count on.
What about you?
Oh, don't worry about me. A noisy place like this is conductive to my creative work.
Creative work... say, the playwright behind La Morte di Texas is named Caterina too. Could you be...?
Uh-huh.
Wow! No wonder you knew so much about the Texas Family!
I read that one! Even I could tell it's one humdinger of a script!
Can I get yer autograph?
Sure. Not that it's worth anything right now.
Le Morty de Texas is gonna be a blockbuster! You'n bet on that!
Thanks.
Back to what you just said...
You look like you can take care of yourselves, and I wouldn't put it past you to actually get it done, if it was any other Siracusan prison.
But things aren't so easy here in Volsinii, with Lavinia around.
The eyes of all the famiglie are on that place. You don't know what trouble you might stir up, if you act rashly.
Heh, we were just kiddin' around.
You didn't look like you were.
May I ask a question?
Okay?
Do you know Cellinia well?
Sit.
There's an unwritten rule in art and writing.
You can base your work on the famiglia, but you can't mention a real famiglia by name.
I've heard that.
But somebody put 'La Morte di Texas' out there.
The first work to use the name of a real famiglia.
And?
That means the famiglie aren't trying to stay in the shadows anymore. They're out to take center stage.
All thanks to the Columbians.
A lot's changed since the Columbians got mixed up in the Siracusan art scene.
Unscrupulous artists—or rather, craftsmen—pumped out work that's starting to stink.
They're not making observations about life, they're making pale imitations of it. As long as audiences are willing to shell out money for it.
It's just a mosaic of pop culture. What was popular yesterday shows up in theaters tomorrow.
Real quality is buried under garbage, which becomes all you find when you look for art.
Isn't that terrible?
If it's what everybody likes, then what you call 'art' sounds like garbage.
Some think like that, but not everyone.
There are those who would like to keep good art from being buried, but they don't know how to fight the tide.
That old timey opera didn't appeal to me.
But you never escaped the past, did you?
Who are you?
Benthonexigios. You'll find my name in the reviews section of Volsinii Arts Weekly.
Ben...
Just call me Ben.
Ben...
I... I've got my thoughts about what Siracusa's become.
People up high say we oughta solve our problems in more civilized ways.
But when I try to do something all civilized, things turn out the same as they always did.
Why do we fool ourselves like that, when we don't actually have to?
The famiglie know how things are trending, but no one wants to give what they already had.
Want a drink?
That wine's not cheap.
It's not.
And this is a cheap pizzeria.
It is.
Why pair a wine like that with pizza like that?
Because the wine is worth the same as the pizza to me.
The wine is certainly finely made, but just as much care was put into growing the wheat that ended up in this pizza.
Is there really that much of a difference in value between them, if you take away the trappings of civilization?
Do you believe in these things?
The trappings of civilization?
Like the label on this wine. Slap it on something, and you're free to feed on the people whom you've fooled.
So Cellinia fled to Lungmen, and you are her friends...
Hard to believe, isn't it?
No, I believe it.
Really?
First, there's no need for you to lie. Second, I always thought there was a chance she was still alive.
Now I understand why she suddenly showed up.
I guess she made a deal with the Bellones, and now she's here to pay her debt.
Definitely sounds like her.
But what a coincidence it is that you found the Compagnia dell'alba... (whispers) or perhaps it's no coincidence...
What's special about that company?
Nothing. It's nothing.
The Compagnia dell'alba is a good place to start, if you want to get close to Famiglia Bellone.
But... well, come with me.
What's this place...?
It's my home.
Wow, books and papers everywhere.
Haha, sorry about that. I didn't have time to clean up.
Take this, Sora.
A key?
Yes. It can be dangerous outside. Come here, if you want to practice.
It's quieter, and I have plenty of scripts you can look at.
But...
Just think of it as making a new friend.
Alright!
Though I considered the possibility that Leontuzzo was trying to pull a fast one on me behind my back, my suspicions were confirmed the moment I saw you.
You're the real deal, a flesh-and-blood Texas.
Allow me to introduce myself. I am Wallach of Famiglia Rossati.
You're Columbian?
Of course. Most associates of Famiglia Rossati are.
So, how did you enjoy prison life here?
There was nothing special about it.
Strange. Logically, you never should've spent so long in jail in the first place. Just what was Bellone thinking?
Is there something unusual about obeying the law?
The law? What do the courts here in Siracusa have to do with the so-called law?
Look at it this way. In most Siracusan cities, standing trial and going to jail is a common coming-of-age ritual for a young mafioso.
After that, his famiglia associates go pick him up, like a warrior returning home triumphant.
Everything on the surface here is meaningless to those who live by the underworld's ways.
What's your point?
You grew up in Columbia. Do I need to spell it out for you?
'A complete joke.' There's no other way to say it.
But that's why we were able to establish ourselves here so easily.
Do you enjoy talking politics?
You obviously don't.
To be honest with you, I'm not a fan either.
I'll get to the point then. Why aren't you dead.
None of your business.
Fine. Let me ask you a different question then. Why did you come back?
Didn't Leontuzzo tell you already? I owe the Bellones a favor.
So, let me get this straight. You knew the Bellones wanted to bring you back to deal with us Rossatis, but you still went along with it.
I didn't know that until I got here, but... you could say that.
Your bluntness makes me wonder if there's something wrong with me, last of the Texases.
Are you going to tell me you forgot the Texas name used to rule all of Columbia's famiglie, large and small?
That your grandfather, Salvadore, was the lodestar for every associate of every famiglia in Columbia?!
What the Texases left behind... has nothing to do with me.
Nothing to do with you?!
Wallach smashes his fist into the coffee table next to him, and fresh blood begins to seep from his fingers.
He does not seem to realize it.
You are Cellinia Texas! The granddaughter of Salvadore!
As long as you live, those things have everything to do with you!
The mere mention of your name at a Rossati table forces us to change the way we do things!
Texas was our pride!
Do you know how much effort Giovanna put in to reunite the remaining Columbian famiglie so that Salvadore's blood and sweat would not go to waste?
Do you have any idea what it was like to finally give in to Signora Sicilia, traveling thousands of miles to go from Columbia to Siracusa?
In order to survive, we even gave up the technology to build a nomadic city, basically letting Siracusa construct a new one for free—
That was all because of the fall of Texas. It left us no choice but to accept this reality.
And now you, the last Texas, deign to set foot here like you own the damned place.
Claiming that you have nothing to do with all this, that you're just here to pay off a debt.
You even go so far as to stand against us!
You owe the Bellones?
That you're even alive means you owe a much bigger debt to the Columbian famiglie!
I might owe Giovanna an explanation, but I don't owe a thing to the Columbians.
After I settle my accounts with the Bellones, I'm leaving Siracusa.
You think we'll take your word for it?
Your very existence shakes the foundations of Famiglia Rossati.
Where's Giovanna?
What?
I'm asking, why are you here? Instead of Giovanna?
I speak for her.
She wouldn't, for example, grovel at Signora Sicilia's feet.
Same as she wouldn't send a representative to meet me.
You think you know her so well?
Judging from your expression, looks like I'm right.
Hah... Isn't that unlucky.
Turns out I'm the one who doesn't know enough about the legendary Texas.
I always thought I was a pretty good actor.
You're not bad.
But, you speak eloquently and at great lengths about Siracusa, as if you've seen through the country for what it is. It feels... very familiar to me.
I wouldn't keep doing that, if I were you.
Why not?
Because it proves... you don't understand this place well enough.
But, you are right about one thing. Now that I'm back, I should go have a chat with Giovanna.
You won't be seeing her.
Sorry about this, last of the Texases.
You're too much like a Siracusan. No, you are a Siracusan, through and through.
So I cannot let you see the Donna.
You'll shake her. And you'll shake others too. You'll bring ruin to a great many things.
So, please turn back.
Go close the deal you have with the Bellones, and then crawl back wherever you came from.
You're free to do what you want from here.
But, I'll ask you to bear one thing in mind.
Famiglia Rossati does not welcome you.
Texas stands up and walks to the door.
Unconsciously, the would-be assassins part and make way for her.
She looks around, reading anger, bewilderment, disgust, confusion, and other emotions in the eyes of the Columbians.
That is what her surname brings to the table.
Texas.
She lets out a sigh and exits the room.
What an unexpected guest.
I want to see Bernardo.
I've already warned you several times before not to call the Don by his first name, Signorina Lavinia.
You know where he is, don't you?
Give me one reason why I should tell you.
If standing here listening to you mock me from your position as the big winner is enough to get you to tell me, I'll gladly play along.
The big winner, huh...?
Whatever.
It's not like you knowing will do any harm. The Don already left for the Saluzzo mansion.
You won't be able to see him.
If you want to stir up trouble in his household, that's on you.
But if the Don has decided to open discussions with the Saluzzos, I suggest you don't stick your head where it doesn't belong.
Don't need to tell me twice.
One more thing, Signorina Lavinia.
I don't care what you think about me.
But, I hope you do remember this.
Leon is a made man in this famiglia, and he was the first to deviate from our path.
We are all one famiglia, and a famiglia ought to stick together.
Your path?
That's a nice euphemism for craven ambition.
Heh.
A droplet of rain strikes the ridge of her eye socket and rolls down like a teardrop.
Never in her life has Lavinia experienced rain so frigid and biting in Siracusa.
But she promptly wipes away the streaks.
It's too soon for that.
Who is it?
I'm looking for Penguin Logistics.
That's me.
Do you have time right now?
I have a moment.
What services does your business offer?
We deliver letters, messages, goods, and... people.
Rain or shine.
Perfect. I have a job for you.
Oh, Sora, you went to Caterina's place after all?
There are tons of resources here, and Miss Caterina even left her notes in a lot of these scripts.
She's really passionate about her job, huh?
*sigh*...
Stop sighin', won'tcha?
Everybody knows yer actin' thingy ain't just another way of chasin' Texas. Leastwise, you don't want it to be.
Right. But, finding her is still our top priority...
After all, Lappland is back in Siracusa, and she's been doing some pretty crazy stuff.
If you think about, it makes perfect sense.
It always feels like she's there, wherever Texas shows up.
Do you think she's doing it to help Texas?
Lookin' at the scoreboard, Texas sure as sunshine got her name cleared. But ain't that kinda strange, whichever way ya look at it?
Yes. I'm sure she has her own goals.
Gaaah, I don't have what it takes to deal with that woman.
Anyways, she ain't no friend of Texas, don't take a genius to know that much. But her attitude ain't much like an enemy, neither.
End of the day, I just can't figure out for the life of me what it is she wants outta Texas.
But, the mille-feuille she gave me earlier was really good.
Don't wanna hear nothin' outta that hungry mouth of yours.
It's kind of like how I used to look at Mostima. I couldn't figure her out, but after I gradually stopped thinking about her, she actually started to make sense to me.
I feel like it's the same with Lappland.
For people like her, there's no point trying to guess at what they're thinking. It's better to just accept what they say at face value.
Only if every word they say is true can you start to figure if they're ultimately trying to weave truths into a lie.
Hmm...
Time for me to get some chow. Exusiai, you watch over things, okay?
You got it~
Sora, are you practicing your lines again?
Oh, hi there, Miss Caterina.
We're about to start our performance of Act II of 'La Morte di Texas,' so I have to step it up.
Oh, I completely forgot. Time sure has a way of sneaking up on you.
Exusiai, would you like some pizza?
Sure would!
By the way, Miss Caterina, since you're the playwright, you'll be coming to watch the show, right?
Hmm...
I actually wanted to tell you that I'm going away for a while.
Huh?
For how long?
Well... It shouldn't be too long, if everything goes as planned.
Don't worry, it's nothing major. Something happened back home, and as the eldest daughter, I have to go back and get everything under control.
Being a big sister is hard work, isn't it?
Haha. It's not so bad.
Unfortunately, I'm still not sure what to do for the climax of Act III.
*sigh*... Maybe I can find some inspiration back home this time.
Alright...
Come on, don't look at me like that. We just won't be seeing each other for a bit. I should still be able to find the time to come watch Act II.
Who knows, maybe I'll surprise you backstage and invite you out.
Really?
After all, I rarely get to make new friends, and I wouldn't want to miss the performance of a script I wrote myself.
Right. That settles it then. If you're coming by, you have to let me know!
Of course!
I'm off then. Goodbye.
Bye bye~
See you later~
Donna, you didn't bring any luggage?
After we're done here, I have to head back to finish my script. What's there for me to bring?
Alright.
How have the Saluzzos and Bellones been reacting?
The Saluzzos invited Leontuzzo to a banquet, and the old wolf Bernardo agreed to it.
That banquet may end up very interesting, and everyone has eyes on it now.
Should we respond in any way?
Wallach, help me make some travel plans.
I'm going to go watch the opening performance of 'La Morte di Texas' Act II.
Is the script you wrote that important to you?
Of course. It is the product of my painstaking labor.
Also, didn't you just ask how we were going to respond?
That is my response, seeing how Bernardo Bellone is about to step away from his own theater.
Naturally, I, Giovanna Rossati, ought to return.
Quartiere Saluzzo
Leon.
I've never been one to tell you what to do. I've always hoped you'd come to your own decisions about everything.
You've done well, all these years.
If you really think that, then why...
Why did you suddenly decide to act now?
Why are you telling me this?
Why... did you betray Lavinia?
Countless questions swirl around Leontuzzo's mind, but none of them leave his lips.
What he just saw and heard seems to contradict his cognition. He suddenly feels as if the man before him is not his father. He's like a stranger.
Because this is the best, and only chance.
What do you mean by that?
You'll find out soon enough.
Signor Bernardo, Signor Leontuzzo, it's a pleasure to see you.
Hmm? You're not one of Saluzzo's associates.
Segretario di Sicurezza Alimentare, Rubio. Why are you...?
My dipartimento is catering tonight's banquet. I'll be cooking, personally.
Oh?
I've heard that in Volsinii, no event worth its salt can be held without the endorsement of il Dipartimento di Sicurezza Alimentare.
You speak too highly of me.
I'm just hoping to make food that satisfies the fine men and women of every famiglia.
Yet another reason to look forward to tonight's banquet.
You came.
Where is he right now?
He went to the Saluzzo mansion.
Okay.
You're not going to ask why?
I can tell that Bernardo is very important to you.
When I became a judge, he didn't ask me to abuse my position to protect the famiglia backing me, like all the other judges.
He let me do what I wanted to do.
Leon, too.
As the Bellone boss in this city, he respects my work and the brand of justice I seek to uphold.
Even if I rule against the Bellones, he sees it as strictly business.
In his words, my justice is more effective than blind violence.
I don't object to his understanding of justice. He even spends some of his spare time reading law literature.
Even so, as a judge, I still have to come face-to-face with the injustices that constantly plague this city.
The power struggles between the famiglie, their oppression of the common folk, and the silent rage of the masses...
The more I tried to uphold justice, the more difficult I realized my path was.
I used to comfort myself that 'at least I'm not the tool of some famiglia.'
But how could that be true? My hands are already dirty.
I just didn't want to want to admit it. It's impossible not to get your hands dirty.
Even if this seems silly to you...
The promise Bernardo made to me was the only reason I was able to remain a judge in Siracusa.
Promise?
He once told me that after Famiglia Bellone emerged victorious, he would carve out a piece of land in Siracusa.
There, the law I placed my faith in would no longer rely on pure power, where the justice I sought could truly be realized.
He's hoped I could be patient until that day comes.
Every time I start to think I can't bear it anymore, I think of his promise.
Tell me, if all of this was a lie, then what purpose has my life had for the past ten years?
I think... given his power and status, he wouldn't need to lie to you like that.
Right? That's why—
Who is it?
Huh? You're—
I am Judge Lavinia. I would like to speak with Don Alberto regarding the murder of Carracci.
Your Honor... The Don is entertaining an honored guest right now and doesn't have time to...
By honored guest, do you mean Bernardo Bellone?
Please inform the two dons that this matter is of great importance.
I will not leave until I see them.
Please try this one. I learned it from a Leithanian chef.
Not bad, not bad.
Times have really changed, Bernardo.
In the old days, whenever we wanted something, it was all: 'I must have it, and I'll kill anyone who gets in my way.'
But now?
I need to find officials or businessmen who are on good terms with me, and ask them to get it for me. Be discreet about it too. Wouldn't want news to get out.
Ridiculous.
Isn't that right, Rubio?
Ah... Anything you desire, can be yours, Don Alberto. For one to be called upon to get what you want is his honor.
Hah.
Segretario Rubio, you must've been plenty lucky yourself to fill that position.
I didn't have that kind of luck, which is why I had to go about it this way. I'm just a clown, currying favor with the bosses.
Heh. Look at this guy, always talking himself down. The fact that you're even here today is enough to prove your worth.
You're right, Don Alberto.
Alright, Bernardo. We've spent enough time exchanging pleasantries, so let's get down to business.
You want to do what the Dodici Famiglie have always wanted to do, but never had the guts—take her down.
And sure. You have my support.
But you've only just muddied the waters. If you really want to overthrow her, you're a long way away.
The fact that you even dared to come to this dinner shows that the cards are still in your hand.
So tell me, what exactly do you plan to do?
Alberto, what do you think about Famiglia Rossati?
Rossati?
They've got ambition.
Famiglia Rossati isn't alive today just because Giovanna's standing up as Salvadore's successor.
Someone must think the legacy of the Texas name—old Salvadore's knack for unification, was an invaluable asset.
If you ask me, it's a liability.
Charisma this, charisma that. In the end, money is what talks. It's the only thing that'll get anyone to give a damn about you.
You're right. And also wrong.
From where we stand, there are always methods of convincing people.
And to be fair, I do somewhat admire Giovanna.
While the Texas legacy did make for a liability, she took it up willingly, and managed to make it her own..
It's true that the Rossatis have depended on the wealth and connections they've accumulated.
But, they've gone too far.
In what way?
Do you really think she wants to hand over the key to the new city to either of our famiglie?
We've been jostling for power here, but the Rossatis haven't shown the slightest interest in participating, as if they're not even here in our world.
But, they are the same Rossatis that took up Salvadore's legacy. They handed over the technology to build that city to Siracusa. Do you really think they're bystanders in all this?
To a new city built to commemorate Salvadore?
A new city that could change the trajectory of Siracusa?
You mind putting it in plain Siracusan for me?
How could I possibly be more direct, Alberto?
The Dodici Famiglie have been battling here in Volsinii. And to her, this is nothing more than a beast-fighting exhibition.
Don Alberto... (*whispers*)
Hmm? Judge Lavinia, and Cellinia?
They want to see me and Bernardo?
What's the meaning of this, Bernardo?
They're not with me.
Oh?
The Saluzzos have never been anything but hospitable to our guests.
But, to those who are not guests—
You can deal with them according to the Saluzzo way.
Haha, alright then. You heard the man?
Yes, Don Alberto.
Bernardo, I know you enjoy chatting about plans, and the future.
But I like more practical topics, myself.
Because you know those like the back of your hand.
The people know the Saluzzos never place a bet lightly. But few remember that when a Saluzzo does place a bet, he doesn't let it go lightly either.
Heh.
Rubio, the rest is not meant for your ears.
I get it—
Segretario Rubio, do you have any interest in opera?
Oh... I sometimes go, when I have the time.
Perfect.
Take this.
This is... a ticket to 'La Morte di Texas' Act II?
Yes. If it sounds like something you'd be interested in, feel free to go check it out.
I told you, Bernardo. The time for pleasantries is over.
I'd also like to invite all Saluzzos and their associates to enjoy the show together.
What are you talking about?
It's time to go.
This is a private party.
And what if I told you I must see the dons today?
The Saluzzos have a particular way of showing hospitality to unwelcome guests.
I'm sorry, Cellinia.
This is what I'm here for, isn't it?
You dare pull your sword on a Saluzzo? Cellinia, last of the Texases, are you outta your mind?
Flip that one around.
To kill, not for the sake of killing, but for an honest and sincere hope—since her return to Siracusa, this is the fight that has felt the least empty to her.
That is what Texas thinks.
My theater manager informed me that Famiglia Rossati has reserved a VIP box for tomorrow night's show.
Oh?
Haha, staging a hit in a theater? Is that how the esteemed artistic director of Compagnia dell'alba operates?
It's just sprinkling the dressing of truth onto a play.
But, there is one thing I still have doubts about.
Giovanna has heavies from Columbia among her associates.
Especially her boss in this city, Wallach. I've heard rumors of his methods.
How are you sure we'll be able to bring her down together?
Don Alberto... (*whispers*)
Haha, sounds like fun.
Bernardo, the judge and that Texas... very much want to see us.
They've already wounded some of my soldiers.
They are both close friends of yours, Bellone. Care to explain?
The associates of Famiglia Saluzzo have always been known for their bloodlust and combativeness.
Alberto, if the Rossatis came to attack your home...
What do you think their chances would be?
Zilch. They wouldn't stand a chance.
Cellinia will be the blade that runs through the heart of Famiglia Rossati.
Oh?
Where Rossati soldiers can't go, Cellinia can.
So, what are you worried about? Can the Rossatis stop her?
I remember you.
I thought you totally gave up on this place, like you never even lived here.
My time with the Saluzzos wasn't so bad.
Then, you gotta know what it is you're doing.
I do.
Texas, you...
Looking at the unconscious mafiosi strewn around her, Lavinia can't help but be briefly lost for words.
Even with all this, Texas was still holding back. She thought she had seen it all in her time dealing with the famiglie, but this is still beyond her comprehension.
I'm glad I'm on your side.
Unfortunately, it looks like this is as far as we'll get.
Huh?
Leon?!
Lavinia... you shouldn't have come.
I have to ask Bernardo something, face-to-face.
The assassination of the boss of Famiglia Rossati has already been decided.
Saluzzo and Bellone will join forces.
That's the decision they made?
It was our decision.
Where is he?
Dinner just ended. My father is upstairs.
You can see him, but nothing will change.
Along the way, Lavinia keeps running over a million different things to say.
There are too many questions she wants to ask Bernardo.
She imagines the countless arguments she'd have with him, as well as their outcomes.
She thinks she has mustered enough anger.
However, when she steps through the door, the scene she encounters shakes her to the core.
Bernardo sits upright in a luxurious chair.
In his hand rests a legal codex adorned with thorns.
The very same as the one she holds in her hands.
He lowers his head and rubs the tome lightly with his palms, as if completely unaware—of the thorns digging into his hands, of the bright blood trickling down.
Her chaotic thoughts all freeze in this moment.
In the end, thousands of words condense into one—
Why?
The game is over, Lavinia.
That sentence alone is already enough for her.
Only a few moments of silence pass, but she is already sapped of all her strength.
Lavinia turns to leave and does not look back, frightened of the tears about to roll from her eyes.
Bernardo remains in place, uttering not a single word as a few drops of his blood dye the floor.
Urgh...
Signor, are you alright?
Just dreaming.
That's why you shouldn't sleep on the sofa.
Where's the old man?
The Don went to the theater.
Are you going too?
Leontuzzo does not answer, only sinking deeper into the sofa.
Rossati, Giovanna.
That's right.
Cellinia, our original agreement was to ensure the Bellones come out on top.
But, I've changed my mind.
As long as you succeed, you don't owe us anything.
At that point, you're free to go.
This, I promise you as the Fang of Zaaro, il Signore dei Lupi.

lines.st: From the time she returned to the residence at sunset, to the break of dawn the next day...
Listening to the falling rain, Texas wiped down her sword while reading the script that was handed to her—'La Morte di Texas.'
The story twists and turns, never straying far from what she's known.
She has a faint premonition.
But first, she needs to sharpen her sword.
Today, it may have to be a bit swifter than usual.

lines.x: Tsk, tsk. The sun's still getting in my eyes, even though it's raining.
Prison food's not too bad. You should give it a try.
You're right about that. I've even given 'em a few suggestions before.
Now get in the car.
Can I sit this one out?
Does killing a single Columbian really deserve so much fanfare?
The Don says Cellinia's gonna be there too.
Oh?
What's the plan?
She'll pretend to be an actress, and once the performance starts, you'll improvise the rest.
Hmm... So what about my costume?
What the Don meant was—
You wait in the audience and play along.
Oh, right. Haha.
What an insensitive old jerk. So where's he gonna be? He's not trying to find a nice place to watch the show all by himself, is he?
The Don has other business. He's got a meeting.
A meeting?
Yeah, with a carwash worker.
Howdy, Sora. I just knew you'd be practicin' in Miss Caterina's studio.
I ain't gonna stop ya, but don'tha think you oughta head to the theater early? To do some makeup n' get ready n' whatnot?
Yeah, you have a point.
Exusiai's already off to buy breakfast. We got just enough time to meet her.
Okay.
Say... Didn't we say we'd set up a big ol' welcome for Exusiai's sister?
We did.
But now I'm not sure we'll have enough time for that.
Croissant, watch your step!
Huh? Wooooah!
Owwie...
Are you alright?
I'm fine, I'm fine. Just tripped on a little somethin'.
Dangit, I knocked over that stack of books. Lemme tidy 'em up.
Huh? A picture dropped outta somethin'.
Picture?
Wait... Huh?
What's wrong?
Lookie here!
It is a photograph from many years ago.
Sora can barely make out the background that should be of the wide and bustling streets of Columbia past.
In the center of the photograph stands a middle-aged man who wears a kind expression, but exudes an audacity not to be trifled with.
On his left and right sides stand a Lupo girl, and a Feline girl.
The girl on the left has beautiful blonde hair and sports a cheery expression, and even at a glance, one can immediately tell that she has a bubbly personality.
The girl on the right has black hair, her face betraying no emotions, making it difficult for one to guess the contents of her heart.
Look, this pitcher... ain't that... Texas, as a kid?
And standin' there with her... That's Caterina?!
And look on the back here...
Written on the backside of the photo: 'Giovanna and Cellinia, 1080.'
Giovanna... Is that gonna be Caterina's real name?
Giovanna... Giovanna Rossati.
How long has this carwash been open?
Five years.
Five years? Coincidentally, that's how long my guard tuskbeast has been missing.
If only I knew how he was doing these days.
The city's a pretty big place. There's always a way to survive.
